reference_output:
rating 5.0. Seems to work fine. I have a Samsung Showcase RH25H5611SR/AA. It is four years old.

This is how I fixed my problem, you might have a different problem.

It is a side-by-side that has a freezer on one side, and the refrigerator on the other.

The freezer worked fine, and the refrigerator was not very cold at all at the top few shelves. Noticed the milk went bad early. Checked with a thermometer and it was like 52 degrees up there.

Took the shelves and drawers out, and removed the cover over the condenser after removing all the screws and using a blow dryer to ease the ice build-up over the entire panel. Careful, there are power connectors to disconnect first in the upper left - tight fit. With the wires still connected, you will only get approximately 45 degrees of movement forward.

The plastic light covers on either side pop off and have a screw behind them. Just work on it with a blow dryer and it should eventually come off. Don't try to force it off. If it still won't come off, check and make sure you don't have any extra screws still securing the panel to the refrigerator.

On the back of the panel you removed, you will see the fan assembly. Opened the black case for the electric motor attached to the fan. It has little snappy things like a lot of cases do. It had water inside it. I then disconnected the electrical connector from the motor.

The fan assembly, which includes a grey plastic housing, is connected by four screws to the back of the main body of the panel you removed from inside the refrigerator.

The motor shaft extends through the plastic fan to the other side, where it is attached with a weak green epoxy, and for lack of a better term, a metal coil around the outside of the plastic. I removed the green epoxy with a dental pick, and used some pliers to gently twist the metal coil off in the opposite direction of how it is coiled.

The plastic where the motor shaft meets with the fan has three points - think of a circle with three notches cut out.

I just took an appropriately sized center punch and pushed on the shaft with one hand, and gently lifted the plastic fan with the other until the fan came off the shaft. The motor will now freely come out of the other side.

The plastic housing the fan moves in (against? Near. Near is good.) has two concentric rings, I put a small amount of white lithium grease where the fan 'could' meet with the housing and rotated the fan against it to spread. This step is not required, and probably not recommended, but I did it anyway.

I put the new motor in the black housing and closed it. I put the fan on the motor shaft to approximate where it was on the old one and replaced the metal coil by gently twisting it into place with a pair of pliers. I did not replace the epoxy, but you might want to put a dab of loctite or something on the end. I figure that if this motor failure is a design flaw and it happens again, I don't want to deal with the epoxy next time.

I tilted the fan to the orientation it will be inside the refrigerator to test it - it needs to rotate freely, so don't jam it down flush with the housing, that's not how it was originally.

After that check, I reconnected the fan housing to the body of the panel.

I then placed the panel back in the refrigerator, and reconnected the power connectors on the upper left.

Replaced the screws and light covers. Replaced drawers and put a thermometer inside on the top shelf.

The new motor fixed the problem I was experiencing. The refrigerator was appropriately cold again.

Huzzah.

It developed a different problem after repair, However. The water from the dispenser would not come out.

What had happened is the bottom of the refrigerator got so cold that it froze the water in the filter. I removed the filter and saw ice crystals. I ran the filter under hot water and dumped it out until the water that came back out was not ice cold.

I don't know if using hot water is bad, you can use cold water if you want, but I was tired of dealing with it all at this point so I used hot water and when I replaced my filter, it worked just fine.

Hope this was helpful.

Review 12: rating 5.0. ... the 2nd time I've purchased these and they fit wonderfully in my humidifier and last quite a while This is the 2nd time I've purchased these and they fit wonderfully in my humidifier and last quite a while. Great product! Review 13: rating 1.0. Not so great Not like the original filter Review 14: rating 4.0. Better than the filter it comes with These are awesome if you know how to use em! But two to use interchangeably... Use one one week, rinse thoroughly at end of week, allow to dry for a week, and use the other one during that week... Then switch back. Et c Review 15: rating 1.0. Not good quality These turn brown in just a few days even after rinsing them after each use. Not good quality at all Review 16: rating 2.0. Molded up with in a week of putting both in Molded almost four days after putting one in, and the same with the second. Review 17: rating 2.0. Not long lasting Doesn't last very long and gets dirty and dry pretty quick. Too bad I didn't know about it when I bought the humidifier. Next time I'll buy a humidifier with a cartridge instead of these filters Review 18: rating 1.0. Grows mold quickly! I followed the directions on the package and still found mold on the filter in just 2 weeks! I was not happy, I bought these filters for my baby's humidifier. Will not be buying again. Frankly, I should have sent them the moldly filters back! Review 19: rating 5.0. Five Stars Exactly what I ordered and works great! Review 20: rating 1.0. One Star Horrible filters. They literally came apart after two uses. Not cool at all.
reference_output: rating 3.0. One Star They put no directions in there and it was kinda hard figuring it out.They can be cut by the way  if you want it to last longer.Hard water will kill it.Make sure you empty inside the humifier where they filter is stored every single day.Now that i got that down if they would put the filters on sale or lower the price i might buy this again when i am ready.

persona: This customer prioritizes functional performance, reliable cooling, and thoughtful packaging when purchasing a compact fridge, and values customizable temperature settings and quiet operation for use in a professional environment.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Five Stars Love it, just perfect for my office. Review 2: rating 1.0. Worked well, died, no refund. This item stopped working within 6 months of purchase. I was denied a replacement. Don't waste your money, spend a little more so you don't throw away 100$. Review 3: rating 5.0. Five Stars Was delivered fast works great.. very quiet Review 4: rating 1.0. hate it. Lots of noise and plastic smell spoils all my drinks ... hate it ... feel like dumping it on the sellers face ... worst product ever Review 5: rating 5.0. works great! I was pretty surprised at how well this little fridge worked out. You just can't beat the price. The fridge is quiet and gets cold quickly. We used it for a trade show and had no problems. It's very light weight and durable at the same time. Great for an office, gym, etc. Review 6: rating 4.0. Four Stars Pretty junky, but it works so far. Review 7: rating 4.0. It arrived timely within a week. Just arrived today ... It arrived timely within a week. Just arrived today. I have in my office at work under desk it is very quiet. I will update within 30 days regarding temp. Review 8: rating 2.0. Wouldn't recommend, read my review I don't think you could fit even one ice cube tray in the freezer department. Big bulge at bottom area takes up lotsa room. Look at photos of how they stack soda cans in there. One shelf removable. Hard to tell which side has handle. Wouldn't recommend. Review 9: rating 5.0. Five Stars I have only used the unit for two months and it it working as advertised! Review 10: rating 4.0. Four Stars Pretty good but wish the temperature setting were better Review 11: rating 4.0. I don't even know what's going on. Ghosts maybe. This fridge has a mysterious power. Whenever it kicks on the cooling it somehow disables my drawing tablet and pen for about 30 seconds. It's a nice fridge but this unintended side effect is very annoying. Review 12: rating 5.0. Nice fridge but noisy Nice fridge but a little noisy. Its in my office so the noise is no worse than the other machines. But I wouldnt place it in an area where you sleep. Made well, very handsome. Works well Review 13: rating 5.0. Perfect size Excellent fridge for home or office use. It's very useful for drinks and small food items. It's very quiet and it stays really cold. The size is perfect especially if your cubicle is limited in space. Review 14: rating 2.0. It sounds like a plane is taking off The fridge came with scrapes on the top of it. It sounds like a plane is taking off. Other than that it is ok. Review 15: rating 5.0. Five Stars I love the size of it, perfect fit for our upstairs living room. Keeps drinks very cold. Review 16: rating 3.0. But this little fridge does sound like a storm or coffee pot brewing For chill affect this fridge gets 5 stars. It definitely does the job on getting your items cold. However, on noise it gets a 2.5 star. Others reported that the device is load and this is true. For me it is bearable because I'm using it in my office so I'm not trying to sleep or keep the area quite. But this little fridge does sound like a storm or coffee pot brewing. Review 17: rating 5.0. Cold! This fridge was actually bigger than I thought it'd be (I didn't pay attention to the size I guess). But it works great and definitely keeps the items in it cold! Review 18: rating 4.0. So far good A bit Noisy but I guess you get used to it, keeps nice and cold, we'll see how long Review 19: rating 2.0. Too loud The fridge was way too loud, so loud to the point where I took the trouble to take this 40 pound fridge to a UPS store to return it. I bought the Danby one instead and it was much quieter, though the Danby one looks less attractive in terms of appearance.

The packaging also made it very hard to pack it back for return. Review 20: rating 3.0. Not suitable for countertop use The description made me think this was going to be far smaller than it actually is; it's not suitable for countertop use, which is what I wanted it for. It's also quite loud.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Keeps stuff cold, good packaging This is a great little fridge that I keep under my desk in the office. It is quiet and keeps my beverages and lunch quite cold. I was able to change the settings until I found the right temp for me. It was also well packaged when I got it.

persona: This customer prioritizes accurate product representation and precise fit for appliance replacements, values functionality and ease of installation, and is willing to overlook minor discrepancies like color mismatches if the part performs correctly and matches the intended function.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. perfect fit. I did not know this was needed. My solenoid valve went bad several years ago so i removed the core and used the needle valve to control flow. Had no idea what I was doing but it worked with frequent checks. This year I replaced solenoid and needle valve. Thought I was through but humidstat did not send voltage to solenoid valve. By reading all the comments for solenoid valve and humidstat, I was able to fix the humidifier with a new humidstat and of course the yellow or... Review 2: rating 4.0. after seven years of use, finally had to change ... after seven years of use, finally had to change the orifice. It fit perfectly. Also might want to change the inline strainer Aprilaire 4004 at this time. Review 3: rating 5.0. works I put a water filter in the system to keep the orifice clear Review 4: rating 4.0. Okay, but... This should come with a replacement hose. I found the old orifice could not be removed; must be glued in. Review 5: rating 5.0. Perfect fit for my Aprilaire 550 humidifier Exact replacement for my Aprilaire 550. Installed this along with a new fill valve. The old orifice was obviously messed up. Now my humidifier no longer has too much water and won't be wasting it all winter. Review 6: rating 5.0. fit like a glove Snapped right in to my valve, fit like a glove. No muss no fuss. Review 7: rating 5.0. Piece was as described and was exactly what I was ... Piece was as described and was exactly what I was looking for. Slight delay in receiving the item as it was on back order for a couple days but that was only issue. Review 8: rating 5.0. In the nick of time! Item came promptly which was good because I had inadvertantly expanded the original by poking a pin through to clear what I thought was a blockage. This resulted in increasing the water flow to the point that the humidifier water tray overflowed! Review 9: rating 5.0. Works great with my Aprilaire 558 humidifier Direct replacement for my original orifice. Came quick and was oem. Review 10: rating 5.0. Suitable replacement for Aprilaire Model 600 orifice This was a suitable replacement for the original orifice in my Aprilaire model 600 humidifier. The old one's hole had enlarged, which caused too much water to enter the evaporator/filter, which then caused leakage outside the case. Review 11: rating 4.0. Four Stars fit fine in our existing tube Review 12: rating 5.0. A perfect fit I love being able to buy a replacement part and install it myself for a tenth of the cost that the furnace guy wanted. Review 13: rating 5.0. Nice filter, works fine This works fine, they are very expensive but, judging by the design, they probably work better, and you only have to replace once a year. Make sure you get it fit in the "slide" top and bottom correctly, and once you get it lined up it should push in pretty easily. Review 14: rating 1.0. Did not receive what was pictured, no instructions, pkging was laughable, did not work I ordered from Amazon partner Complete Filtration Services Inc.
The piece I received was yellow like the photo but the dimensions did not match the photo. It was about 3.5X as long as it was wide. No instructions included. I assume due to the excess length, once inserted in the outlet tube I could not get the ferrule and compression nut to seat at the outlet of the solenoid. So I tried install... Review 15: rating 5.0. Perfect fit for Aprilaire 550 humidifier. The old oriface used so many gallons of water that it cost me $50-60 more per month on my water bill. Very easy to replace and fit perfectly. Notice in the picture how much larger the hole in the middle of the old oriface is. You can barely see the hole in the new one. Review 16: rating 2.0. Beware The product I received was about twice the length of the OEM orifice. The length means it has to slide down past the constriction caused by the compression fitting on the pipe, and, at least in my case, it would not. It was a bit of a pain trying to trim this tiny thing to length, but once I did, it works just fine.
reference_output: rating 1.0. Item received was not what was pictured I'm not sure what the orifice I received was supposed to fit but it certainly was completely different than the picture.  I attached a picture of what I received.  It's blurry but you can see the general size and shape.

persona: This customer prioritizes product accuracy and compatibility, specifically seeking genuine replacements that fit their equipment perfectly; they value convenience, reliability, and competitive pricing, often choosing to purchase online despite potential shipping costs to avoid the hassle of in-store shopping.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Love I have an OXO On Barista Brain 12-Cup coffee maker and these fit perfectly! I previously tried the 12 cup Bunn filters, which worked, but seemed a little too bulky or oversized. These were also cheaper than other brands and had a higher count! The packaging was a little unconventional, but for the quality and price, who cares! Will be ordering again for sure!

Review 2: rating 1.0. Coffee and they were perfect but these are about three times bigger and don't ... We have been using the Melitta  8-12 cup basket filters in our Mr. Coffee and they were perfect but these are about three times bigger and don't come close to fitting. Never, never again!!!!

Review 3: rating 1.0. Too expensive WAY too expensive.  We just bought them on a whim b/c we forgot to get at our local grocery.

Review 4: rating 4.0. No more gounds in my coffee. Yaaayyy The bottom diameter was a little larger than I expected but, other than that, these are great.  No more coffee grounds spilling over the edge and getting into my coffee when I make a full 12 cups of coffee. :-)

Review 5: rating 4.0. Great for OXO Works perfectly in OXO 12-cup Barista Brain.

Review 6: rating 5.0. Just as described. Arrived when they said. Just as described.  Arrived when they said.

Review 7: rating 1.0. I have a 12 Kitchen Aid Automatic Coffee Maker. ... I have a 12 Kitchen Aid Automatic Coffee Maker.  These filters are way to large. Must be for commercial use.

Review 8: rating 5.0. Five Stars Perfect! Great quality & awesome customer service

Review 9: rating 5.0. Twice as many for half the price So glad I found these filters to replace the Oxo 12-cup filters I had been buying for my Technivorm Moccamaster coffee maker. I love that these are unbleached, and for 1/2 the price, there are twice as many filters. These filters seem to be just a little bit thinner than the Oxo filters, but hold up well and perform as they should. Quick shipping and truly appreciated that there wasn't any superfluous packaging, as with so many things from Amazon. Wil...

Review 10: rating 5.0. Five Stars This is the second time I ordered these they work well!

Review 11: rating 5.0. Wonderful Nice and large finally have a filter that fits right for a 12 cup pot. No more filter folding in. Coffee also has a better taste with using these natural filters, very happy with them.

Review 12: rating 5.0. Commercial Size & Unbleached These are the same size as Bunn commercial filters but are unbleached for your coffee-drinking pleasure!

Review 13: rating 1.0. One Star The filters cane in flat. It makes it hard to keep them from folding when making coffee.

Review 14: rating 3.0. Three Stars Too big but I cut some off edge and still using them

Review 15: rating 5.0. Good value, great filter I bought these to use in my OXO Barista Brain 12 cup coffee maker and they fit perfectly.

Review 16: rating 5.0. Five Stars These are perfect for our OXO coffeemaker. They don’t fall apart after brewing.

Review 17: rating 5.0. Five Stars What can really said about coffee filters!

Review 18: rating 5.0. Perfect size for a large basket. Really pleased with the quality. We dug an old, unused coffee maker out of the basement. There were NO filters available in the grocery store that were big enough for the basket. These are PERFECT!!!

Review 19: rating 4.0. A bit large, but usable I like these filters as they are biodegradable, but do not like that they are more like the industrial size "12-cup" than the regular home '12-cup" size. I will continue to use them, to help in any way I can with reducing waste, but wish their base was a tad smaller as to fit home coffee brewers a littel better.

Review 20: rating 5.0. Perfect fit for OXO Barista Brain 12-cup! I love that I finally found filters to use for my OXO Barista Brain coffee maker. These fit perfectly. It's nice that they are the natural unbleached too.  I think they are a teensy bit less sturdy than the OXO brand ones, as once in a while the edge of the filter wants to fold down a bit.  I just take an extra second and make sure all sides are standing tall.  So if I could give it a 4.5 I would for that reason. But with the great price, I'm...
reference_output: rating 5.0. Work great for the large Bunn coffee makers I bought these for my husband to take to work, as folks decided they wanted to stop using the bleached filters. These were convenient and just what we were looking for. These fit the larger coffee makers and work just great. Very pleased with these filters.

persona: This customer prioritizes cost-effective, reliable solutions that deliver proven performance, often choosing third-party or alternative parts based on trusted customer feedback, and values functional accuracy over brand or aesthetic appeal.
reviews:

Put in two Honeywell hygrometers that I use In my humidors and they were exactly 72% humidity. Review 4: rating 3.0. Good humidity sensors... Use a couple of these for a snake habitat and so far they're ok. I've compared the temperature readings to some calibrated thermostats and temperature guns and these have been off by as much as 8 degrees! Granted it's been more accurate but still off by as much as 5 degrees. As humidity sensors, they're great and very spot on. Make sure you place them within good view because you can't really see them from the sides. You have to look at them directly to be ab... Review 5: rating 1.0. Wildly different readings while both in the same humidor. I have both of these sitting in a humidor. While I could certainly expect a few % difference, this isn't even close. Not sure what I'm going to do at this point as far as returning. Review 6: rating 1.0. Do not buy. Highly Inaccurate. Completely wrong readings. Placed both 'cheap' hygrometers as well as a more expensive one in a bag with a boveda 75% humidity control test. My more expensive one was a perfect 75%. The cheap Chinese made hygrometers registered 68% and 72% and there is no way to calibrate. Review 7: rating 1.0. One Worked/ One was Junk I really only needed one and the 1st one I pulled out of the 2pk was junk. I figured it out quickly. When you have 4 thermometers and hydrometer and one of the 2 new meters is 20% off humidity and 15° off temp. That would/could have killed my chicks!
The other one worked great out of the box!
I gave 1 star because of the massive bad read on the 1st one that I was going to use. And if I never checked it before I used it, the consequences could have b... Review 8: rating 3.0. Not very accurate I used to think it was accurate but it's been reading really high humidity and so I brought out the other one, and it's 10% higher, so they don't even match at all. It's 0% humidity outside and I'm running my portable air conditioner, so that made me suspicious about the accuracy. Review 9: rating 1.0. Waste of money. Very inaccurate. Inaccurate at least for temperature. I purchased the two pack for my gecko terrariums. I have one older thermometer but I liked the hydrometer feature on these. Older thermometer and the room thermostat are in sync with each other and generally show the same temp. These are way off. Thermostat and old thermometer show ambient temp in the room to be 66 degrees. This thermometer says the temp IN the HEATED terrarium is 64.9 degrees. I can stick my hand ... Review 10: rating 1.0. Measurements are wrong They are not good folks. Don't buy it. Both ones I received are unreliable. Review 11: rating 2.0. Not accurate These aren’t accurate. The humidity seems fine, compared to two other calibrated humidor hygrometers, but the temperature is 5 degrees lower than the reading on the humidor hygrometers. Review 12: rating 2.0. accuracy doubful placed directly next to each other and the humidity reading is 2% difference Review 13: rating 1.0. Cheap inaccurate product. DO NOT USE IN INCUBATOR OR OTHER HUMIDITY OVER 70%. Item failed (display and accuracy) in the incubator at a humidity of 75%. Review 14: rating 5.0. Accurate, easy to read, bargain! These seem to work very well. We use them in our reptile tank to check the temperature of the basking side and the cooler side. I checked them against the weather channel's outside temperature and it was exactly the same. The double sided foam tape provided holds them onto the aquarium with no issues. The only thing I'd change would be to make the temperature the bigger font and the humidity the smaller number. Review 15: rating 1.0. REVIEW UPDATE 8/2020 **** JUNK DO NOT BUY **** AFTER SEVERAL MONTHS OF OWNERSHIP, THESE AEW JUSR TOTAL JUNK. NOWAY TO ADJUST OR RESET. THEONLY THING PTHATP WORKED IS THAPT IT COST ME A FULL HUMIDOR OF 50 GOOD CIGARS DUE TO IMPROPER HUMIDITY. ****JUNK, DO NOT BUY **** Review 16: rating 2.0. One reads 10 and the other 23 (not sure why those numbers) but neither will change. If I take batteries out and back in, they’ll work for about 30sec and go back to those numbers. Review 17: rating 5.0. Perfect product, not so much perfect delivery Everything seems good. They work well and seem accurate. They look sturdy and exactly how pictured.

Definitely not a product problem, but idk what is up with amazon lately. I live in an apartment complex now. I've ordered 3 things so far. The first package was just set underneath the mailboxes downstairs, nowhere near my door. The second time the package was left on my doorstep, picture was taken, and I recieved a knock at the ... Review 18: rating 2.0. Humidity readings seem to be around +/- 30% or so. Tempature stays accurate. Good for tempature, poor for use in high humidity enviroments Review 19: rating 4.0. It serves it's purpose and for less than $5 Small and mostly accurate. Button cell battery included with each one. I would give 5 stars but it arrived with batteries inside and it has no on/off switch meaning it was operating while in transit. For some reason, that was mildly annoying to me but may be a perk for someone else. Overall, a good product. Review 20: rating 4.0. Great value 11/21/19 review.
Small convenient meter, within .4 of a degree of temperature based on my calibrated temperature gauge and withing. 5 % of humidity.
Has a battery compartment on the back that could use a better clasp but light duty use should be fine. Not recommended for water splash or immersion locations.
Devices come with double sided tape for back, will block battery compartment though.
Clear display although the temp is roughly 50% smaller than th...
reference_output: rating 5.0. Accurate and useful This two-pack is a real bargain. I have a few of the air-tight acrylic cylinder containers for cigars, and these hygrometers ensure the humidity is spot-on. Don't waste your money on more elaborate hygrometers. I also put one of these in my nice wooden humidor that has an analog hygrometer on the front. It's amazing how off that one is.

persona: This customer prioritizes exact compatibility and OEM-quality replacements, values clear installation instructions, and prefers durable, functional parts that offer a straightforward upgrade over full unit replacements.

The smallest adapter was a hair to small and the other too big I cant use them because they wont fit with either adapter - ones too big the other is a bit too small. I may take a dremel and ream out the smaller one a tad. I think if the adapter fit I would be satisfied. Review 16: rating 1.0. Not universal Not universal as advertised.. Didn't fit my GE stove Review 17: rating 1.0. Not worth it None of the nobs fit they were mix match and some pieces were missing. Review 18: rating 5.0. Funtional and attractive Exactly what I needed to serve as the missing knobs for my baseboard heaters - had to insert the chosen cylinder into the knob and use a hammer to affix it all to the metal heater dial post, however, that worked and the knobs are perfect. Review 19: rating 5.0. Universal Window Ac Knob. This is so Great, my Whirpool Window ac is more than 15 year old with no part # and to be available to find the right knob is Great. Review 20: rating 5.0. Highly Recommend We needed this to replace a broken knob in our older freezer model. This works great for all sorts of general uses and it’s pretty sturdy. I was thankful to find something like this with multiple part pieces to guarantee it would fit what I need. The price is also amazing for the quantity of good quality pieces they sell in one bag.
reference_output: rating 5.0. Saved our spin dryer The timer knob broke on the mini spin dryer we use for drying bathing suits and we had no luck finding a replacement. This control knob however, was just the right size. The 4 pack comes with 2 different length adapters that accommodate a 1/4 or 5/16" shaft with a flat spot. The shaft on our dryer is round with a pin through it but I was able to drill the adapter to round it out then cut a notch to slide it over the pin. This knob is sturdier than the original one but even if it breaks, I have 3 spares!  It fits snug and now the dryer is useable again.

persona: This customer values practical functionality and cost-efficiency, preferring solutions that reduce ongoing expenses like bottled water while delivering reliable performance, and is open to trying new products with moderate expectations, often making purchases with both personal use and gifting in mind.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 4.0. helps "white dust" I was hoping the white dust would be eliminated, but we have VERY hard water, so it helps, but doesn't elminate. Review 2: rating 1.0. Water cartridge I have seen zero difference with a before and after comparison. I am using it in a brand new humidifier. For me personally it’s a waste of money Review 3: rating 5.0. Do not buy In my experience with isinlive Demineralization Cartridges, I find them totally ineffective. Day 3 of use, decided to pretreat the water with one Isinlive Demineralization Cartridge in a gallon of water for 12 hours prior to adding the water to the humidifier - result is no reduction to the amount of white powd... Second test was to clean surfaces and humidifier, let two new tabs sit in full tank for 24 hours, and run. Still left mineral dust on surfaces. Final test; clean surfaces and humidifier, FOUR tabs in water tank... Review 9: rating 1.0. Doesn’t work still has a lot solid deposit on humidifier Easy to use but it doesn’t work, still has decay on the humidifier. Review 10: rating 2.0. Not as expected I purchased these and put them two in the humidifier on February 7. Today is March 5 and a few days ago I started to notice white powder on the lenses of my glasses which I keep on the bedside table at night. The instructions said they would last about 90 days. It been less than 30 days. Review 11: rating 1.0. These do not prevent white dust I put two of these in my humidifier. They did not remove any minerals from the water my humidifier still left white residue all over everything. If you have a Ionizer humidifier the only thing that works is distilled water. Review 13: rating 1.0. junk several of these leaked a large amount of tiny bits of what appears to be charcoal. that in turn got ingested into the water system in the humidifier reservoir and damaged the motor and transducer assembly. ruined a humidifier. won't buy again. Review 14: rating 2.0. White dust! Did not eliminate any white Dust, even though I put 4 of them in 1.5 gallons of water.
The white dust is awful, it especially clings to eye glass and I had to keep cleaning them.
The white dust clings to any eye glasses within reach of the water vapors. Review 15: rating 1.0. Doesn't Work After a few weeks of using these cartridges, I've found that they don't work at all. The Homedics brand cartridges actually had mineral buildup inside of the cartridge, as to be expected with my hard water. Now the mesh screen on these look clean and there's a nice white film covering the exterior of my humidifier.

DO NOT BUY! Review 16: rating 1.0. White dust persists. Didn’t work in large tank. House covered in white dust. Review 17: rating 3.0. Seem to help cut down on white dust a little bit. I put two in my 1-1/2 gallon humidifer but there's still white dust forming. The cartridges float so I had to put them in a small container of water and shake them under water until they would sink to the bottom then I put them in the humidifer afterwards and they sank to the bottom. I think the cartridges have trapped air inside and it makes them float. I'm doubtful that I will buy these again.
reference_output:
rating 3.0. Demineralization cartridge Works as advertised. Use in my dehumidifier. Appears to help reduce the white dust. I needed to add additional capsules to reduce more. I am still adjusting to find the magic number. Only used about a month thus far so unsure if there will be a long term affect on machine.

It is nice not buying jugs of distilled water.

persona: This customer prioritizes product reliability and long-term performance over initial cost, willing to invest in higher-quality replacements when cheaper alternatives fail, and values functional, durable parts that solve specific repair issues despite minor installation adjustments.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Perfect fit - stopped the water leak I have a 2013 Whirlpool WDF 750SAYBC dishwasher with the diverter motor in the center of the tub. This part is not shown on the Whirlpool parts diagram, so it was only because of YouTube videos and Amazon that I could track down the part number and how to replace it. It took about 15 minutes to disassemble the sprayers to get to the sump, drain the sump, and pry the old seal out. I ran the dishwasher three times and no leaks. I'm going to purchase... Review 2: rating 5.0. Worked great to fix a leak I bought this to fix our Whirlpool dishwasher that was leaking. I needed to replace the grommet near the diverter motor and hopefully stop the leak. This was the cheapest I could find, including shipping, and it worked beautifully. It shipped and arrived quickly and the dishwasher was fixed in 10 minutes. Review 3: rating 5.0. Be sure this is the cause of leak and then give it a try. Works perfect This worked perfectly on my Whirlpool WDT790SAYM1. Arrived quickly and was easy to replace. Once replaced, it stopped the leak from my dishwasher.
It's a shame that Whirlpool has become so greedy that it's not selling the cheap replacement part and wants to sell the whole bloody SUMP (At least 5 times the value of this small grommet).
Check the leak before you order. If it's in the bottom middle of your... Review 4: rating 5.0. This one has a stainless garter spring. Used this seal to repair my leaking (again) KitchenAid KDTM704ESS0 dishwasher. Was impressed to see this seal is the same dimensions as the original, and that the garter spring is non-magnetic stainless. Since I didn't have to worry about the spring corroding, I was able to install the seal the proper way, with the garter side up, so the water pressure will actually assist with the sealing, rather than act against it. I expect this seal to w... Review 5: rating 4.0. Good Fit for Whirlpool MDT910SAYM1 The grommet is not compatible to Whirlpool dishwasher MDT910SAYM1. It is just too big for the hole. I tried press fitting it using a flat washer and a pipe as a tool to somehow force it in, but the grommet is so rigid and just too big for the hole. If someone can provide the correct size, I greatly appreciate it.

Update 5/2/2020: I figured out why it doesn't fit. The worn out existing grommet has a metal base. This piece must be rem... Review 6: rating 1.0. This just sits in place, and is easy to remove. Review 7: rating 1.0. Faulty unit or piece of junk Within 1 week of installing this, the "spring" inside was completely red and flaking rust. Like most of the other reviewers my dishwasher started leaking and pooling water on the floor in front of it. I removed the toe kick to investigat... Review 20: rating 1.0. Said it was the replacement..wrong! Said it was the replacement, leaked had to use old grommet...
reference_output: rating 1.0. Kitchenaide dishwasher.  Stopped leak for a few days, then leaked again Item worked to stop the leak for a few days, then one day we're swimming again.  Before inserting, I greased it with silicone and wrapped white plumbers tape (Teflon) around the outside as others suggested.  After  a few days, the leak came back.  Returned this one and purchased the more expensive Orange gasket $25 and so far no leaks.  I think the mold used to make these just is not exact enough and while all the cheap gaskets on Amazon look similar, I've gone through three so far from various venders and ONLY the orange gasket seems to be holding.  So, spend the extra $15 and get the orange one, your time is worth it.

reference_output: rating 5.0. Perfection!! These are a perfect replacement for the "chrome" ones that came on my cooktop. I like the black contrast which matches the knobs and hides the goo between cleanings.

persona: This customer prioritizes functional design and performance in kitchen and laundry appliances, specifically seeking compact yet powerful solutions with high efficiency and strong technical capabilities, and is willing to accept minor trade-offs like cleaning effort or space constraints for superior results.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 3.0. Works GREAT but beware.... The product works as advertised. After installation it cut my drying time from 2-3 hours to around 40 minutes. Unfortunately, when we have a windy day(which is often in Oklahoma) the fan turns on because it thinks the dryer is running. I emailed the manufacturer but haven't received a response. Kinda wish I had bought one which is operated with a wall switch. Review 2: rating 5.0. Fantech Dryer Booster EXCELLENT!

Installed it outdoors about 15' from dryer. Lowers drying time by 45 min.

And, made in the USA! Review 3: rating 5.0. An Exceptional Product After purchasing my new home, I discovered that my clothes dryer took much longer to dry. Based on the location of my laundry room and the design of my home, the absolute shortest dryer run about 35 feet with three 90° angles. Keep in mind that each 90° angle is equal to approximately 5-7 feet of straight run pipe. After discussing with a contractor, I found out that I needed a dryer booster fan based upon the venting distance.

While researching m... Review 4: rating 5.0. Works great!! I am very pleased with this purchase. I own a 40+ year townhome in North San Diego County and I have experienced so much trouble with clothes drying. I recently had my washer and dryer moved to a totally different wall and my plumber mentioned this product to me. He heard about it from another client.
Well, I figured what the heck! I have tried just about everything and I purchased it.
It works great. My towels dry in 30 minutes. I have waited a month to... Review 5: rating 5.0. exhaust booster fan We had trouble drying for years because our contractor had an undersized vent (for the length) to the dryer. This item installed easily, and has worked well. The extraction fan pulls the dryer vent exhaust out and we can dry is far less time, and with less electricity. easy to install, automatic to use. All you need is 110 vac somewhere in your attic. Review 6: rating 4.0. Works as advertised, monor problems I investigated the fan because my dryer was installed on an interior wall so the duct ran about 30' with 3 elbows, lent built up & cloths took a long time to dry. I installed the fan in my attic about 20' from the dryer. I used duct tape initially because I had a hard time finding the FC Claps used for reduced virbration. The fan is sensitive to air pressure & starts easy. When I open a window in my kitchen on a windy day the fan will start without... Review 7: rating 3.0. Works OK, but the support was a bit flimsy The fan works as advertised and wasn't very difficult to install, but the support bracket is very flimsy. I built a small frame out of 2x4s to give in more support. Also, the compartment where the AC wire attaches is too small and is poorly designed. The terminal strip works ok for attaching NM-B solid wire; but it does not accommodate connecting an AC power cord (with stranded wire) that is plugged into an outlet. I had to attach little... Review 8: rating 2.0. trouble this is the second one the first one we orderd for customer wouldn't shut off talked to tech support they told us to order new one and send other back we are waiting for our customer to get back from vacation to install replacement well let you no tks Review 9: rating 4.0. Still going..... Still going strong.

I had an issue where the fan started making a very loud humming noise.

I thought it was going to need replacement, since it had been 2 years. But just for grins, I decided to call the manufacturer anyway... Turns out there is a 5 year warranty, which I was happy to hear!

In the end, it turned out that the fan was just clogged with lint (even though I have an additional lint trap between the dryer and the fan, bu... Review 10: rating 3.0. Get ready for some DIY work I received my DBF 110 two days ago and installed it yesterday. There were/are problems.

First, the instruction manual says install it at least 5 feet away from the dryer - OK, no problem. Then next page says it MUST be installed at least 15 feet away from the dryer. Problem; I have less than 15 feet total run from the dryer to any place that I can mount it. I was on the verge of returning it, but I really wanted it to work, so I began the instal... Review 11: rating 5.0. Worth every penny! Dry times are now down to 40-60 minutes vs. nearly 2-3hrs on large loads! UPDATED 8/6/15:

Still LOVE this unit, and it has continued to work flawlessly! I had marked my phone's calendar for the one year mark since installation as a reminder to crawl under the house (where I installed it) take it apart and clean it, clean the exhaust vent as well (as best as I could) and put it back together as general maintenance. I'm glad I did, as the unit had some b... Review 12: rating 4.0. Great And Quite Fan For years we have been having to run our dryer almost two times for each load to adequately dry items. After some research online, I realized our dryer vent duct run was 20ft which was way more than it should be without having an in-line booster fan. I looked for sometime at many brands and I decided to purchase this one due to the reviews that this unit was really quite. I was amazed that the various manufacturers don't mention how noisy or quite their units are.... Review 13: rating 5.0. Made a vast improvement I knew for years that the original design of this house was a screwy deal for the dryer. The laundry room in the middle of the house with a 34 foot run to the outside thru the attic. Convenient for laundry but inefficient for the dryer until now (plus would go thru dryers trying to move air all that distance). The drying times now are more than halved by using this product. Should have done it years ago. Review 14: rating 4.0. LINT TRAP STRONGLY RECOMMENDED My dryer exhaust duct runs about 20 feet and I had moist lint clogging the duct, and water dripping form the joints. I've also gone through 3 dryers in 10 years due to the strain of pushing that moist heavy air. This fan seems to have solved that moisture and water drip, but approximately 6 weeks after install the fan began to vibrate badly. It's mounted to a floor joist in my crawlspace and it shook the whole bottom floor of the house. The problem i... Review 15: rating 4.0. Had one of these in a previous house. It ... Had one of these in a previous house. It helped considerably where we had to design the exhaust path after the house was built. Installed this one as a result of a remodel. This one works well to move dryer exhaust/lint out of the house/garagee and reduces drying time. Only downside: I thought this one came with a power cord already connected, but was wrong, and it added time to the install process. Review 16: rating 5.0. Throughput for laundry markedly better, and I expect significant savings in dryer run ... House built in 2001 had dryer duct under slab and out to front of house for total run of some 25 feet with at least 4 elbows in the 4" PVC. Couple of dryers "never worked well". I read about duct length / flow problems and saw the booster fan option. Installed this unit in a "push down" configuration otherwise following installation instructions. The drying times are probably... Review 17: rating 5.0. If Your Dryer Needs a Boost Find it Here Our house was built with a really long run of dryer vent through the basement. We replaced a bunch of cruddy old PVC (not code!) with nice steel duct work, took some of the bends out, but it was still inefficient. The dryer was old, so we figured maybe it had run out umph, but its replacement was not significantly better. We were working on all kinds of other solutions when somebody suggested we look at a booster fan. D'uh!

After re... Review 18: rating 5.0. Highly recommended, makes your dryer work the way it should. This thing is great. It should be mandatory to have these on dryer runs over 15 ft. Now I can exhaust a dryer where I want and it's better than if the dryer's on a outside wall. Starts when your dryer starts and stops when your dryer stops. Almost every dryer installation I've seen (and I'm a remodeler) has been poorly done and run much longer than the dryer is capable of pushing. This is the blower the dryer manufacturers ... Review 19: rating 4.0. The package arrived intact and everything was there and nicely packaged. Reading through the instructions the installation seeme I have a long horizontal dryer duct run and it routinely gets clogged with lint. Finally did something about it and bought this booster fan along with the Fantech Lint Trap. The package arrived intact and everything was there and nicely packaged. Reading through the instructions the installation seemed straightforward. I agree with another reviewer's commen... Review 20: rating 4.0. buyer beware (updated) purchased product and had it professionally installed. 20 months later the product stopped working! quite disappointed in product lifespan. manufacture says it has a 5 year warranty;but, the caveats make it difficult to replace under warranty without MUCH expense (paying to have the product uninstalled AND shipped back; the inconvenience and cost of having the duct work temporarily redone to accommodate the missing fan; and paying to have the unit reinstalled u...
reference_output: rating 5.0. Must have for long vents This did the trick.  My dryer is on an inside wall.  The vent goes about 15' up the wall into the attic then about 25' across the top of the garage to vent out the side of the house.  It was taking 2 1/2 hrs per load for normal clothes and 3 to 5 hrs for jeans, towells, etc to dry.  Now it takes 30 to 45 minutes max!!  Highly recommend if you have a long vent.  I installed this in the attic and it runs very quiet.  I can hear it for a few minutes pulling air through the dryer a few minutes after the dryer shuts off.  I did have to make the sensor a little less sensetive.  It was turning on I guess from wind blowing through the vent from the outside.  a small adjustment and all is working great.

The only thing, I don't like is that it's digital, so if it accidentally gets unplugged in the middle of a cycle, you have to start over. Review 9: rating 1.0. ... machine cleaned the dishes well and I was very happy with it This machine cleaned the dishes well and I was very happy with it, but it was completely inoperable after a couple of months. The controls wouldn't light up at all, so I figured it was just a fuse. The problem is, this unit doesn't have any stand-alone fuses. No protection at all, so it will blow a control board. Thankfully I still had time to return it for free through Amazon. A couple more weeks and I would have ... Review 10: rating 4.0. Highly recommend I'm really pleased with this purchase. You can fit 6-7 mugs/glasses, tops. But I still manage to only have to run it about once a week and haven't had any problems with leaking or otherwise after about 2 months. Review 11: rating 3.0. Review 15: rating 5.0. How did I live before? I live in a tiny house with no diswasher. One day something snapped and I decided I was tired of doing dishes and I began the search for a portable dishwasher. I owned a portable dishwasher in the past that worked out well, so this was not really a scary venture for me. I have no idea why I it took so long for me to get the idea and reinvest. Now I'm not sure how I lived all this time. Because it's just me and my son half time, the size is perfect and it h... Review 16: rating 5.0. Well, we bought a different portable first, but that ended up leaking and being too wide not to block access to one lower cabinet. This one is slim, yet st... Review 17: rating 3.0. portable dishwasher This dishwasher was delivered with a big dent in the side of it. However, it works well and is a perfect size for my needs. It moves around very easily. It was packed well and I was disappointed at the damage. Because it works I will keep it, though. It was $50 cheaper than the others like it advertised on Amazon. Review 18: rating 5.0. Love it. It does a really great job Love it. It does a really great job. There are only two of us in my home and it is the perfect size for the amount of dishes I dirty during the day. It even fits the pots and pans I use when cooking dinner. It has different settings. I use all in one tables, and the dishwasher has a setting for their use. I think it would be great for anyone who has limited space, or for just a couple. Review 19: rating 5.0. This dishwasher works great. Easy and quick setup This dishwasher works great. Easy and quick setup. All you have to do is connect it to your sink, plug it in, turn on the hot water, select the cycle, and add soap and you're good to go. The normal cycle takes 1hr 55 minutes, but there are slightly longer and shorter cycles depending on what you are washing. For a portable dishwasher it has a lot of room. At 36" tall it is the same height as a normal countertop which is nice ... Review 20: rating 5.0. Perfect! Love it! I have a small kitchen and had no space in install a dishwasher. I keep this in my laundry room and roll it out as needed. You can fit a good bit of dishes in it and it cleans very well!! Spotless! Better than any dishwasher I've ever had. Very easy to hook up to the faucet. Just turn the water on press start and let it run. Love it love it!
reference_output: rating 3.0. Arrived in pristine condition. Set up, plugged in ... Arrived in pristine condition. Set up, plugged in, ran a rinse cycle, it leaked. 😢. Return was SO uncomplicated and refund was almost immediate.  Decided it was smaller than I wanted.

reference_output: rating 1.0. 6 months to failure I ordered this to replace the OEM pump that quit working after many years of service.  6 months in, the water stops pumping out.  Upon inspection, the impeller came off the shaft. Its made of very cheap plastic and appears to have flashing left in two of the three windows that look like they should be punched out.  One broke through and may be throwing it off balance.  I was able to snap it back on but nothing holds it in place except for an o- ring and will retry but not holding my breath that it will last more than a few weeks.  This should last way longer that 6 months and could if not made of very thin cheap plastic.

persona: This customer prioritizes superior performance and ease of cleaning in coffee filters, values durability and strong coffee extraction, and prefers reusable metal filters over paper or mesh alternatives, showing a willingness to accept minor fragility in exchange for better functionality and convenience.
reviews:
Review 1: rating 5.0. Great filter I had been looking for a good reusable coffee filter for some time and found this one. I love that its easy to clean and way sturdier than a plastic filter I had! Review 2: rating 5.0. Good Fit & Finish When my new coffee machine came w/o a filter basket (a questionable cost reduction), I went on-line to look at aftermarket "universal" filter baskets and there were a bunch of them. I selected this one because it was metal and I liked its grill pattern. It was a perfect fit for my Mr. Coffee machine, easy to clean. Its grill pattern is small engough to trap particles but flows well enough to prevent back-ups. I like the hinged basket handle. For a slight cost p... Review 3: rating 5.0. Quality product This item was purchased to replace a plastic mesh filter basket. This stainless steel basket is well-made and should last for a long time. It cleans up perfectly in the dishwasher, and takes a little bit of effort to clean up by hand. Review 4: rating 5.0. Solved my paper filter woes. I bought this for two main reasons. I was sick of throwing filters away and was sick of them falling over during the brewing cycle resulting in grounds in my cup. This reusable options solves both of those issues. The holes are the perfect size for water to slowly get through without grounds. I’m going to buy a couple more to rotate! Review 5: rating 5.0. BEST EVER!!! My husband love this product. Easy to clean, Sturdy and filters coffee perfectly. I recommend this product wholeheartedly. Review 6: rating 5.0. It's super easy to clean, and fits perfectly inside my Mr. Coffee machine. It's also not just eco-friendly, but practically paid for itself after a couple weeks of not having to buy messy paper filters. Review 7: rating 5.0. Well constructed Fit and finish is really good,however may be a little porous and will just use a paper filter under it.Coffee does not stain the stainless steel,just have to wash it after each use. When you put the pot in it pushes the valve up into the filter basket and raises the filter. The re... Review 13: rating 4.0. No grounds in coffee! Product works very well. This not only saves money ,does a good job, but saves me from running out of paper filters when I forget to buy them. The only negative is, it seems to not empty old grounds as easy as I had hoped. Worth the money I'm a avid coffee drinker and hate paper filters collapsing having coffee grounds in my coffee. I've used the plastic permanent filters as well. But the metal one will pay for itself and a breeze to clean up. Never have to worry about coffee grounds in your cup or mug again. Review 15: rating 5.0. Love this reusable coffee filter! Between the extra waste and half the time a side falls in and I get grounds in my coffee...hate them. The first time we used this filter, my husband and I both realized the flavor was better. The holes are small enough that neither of us noticed any grounds or residue IN our coffee. So nice. Dump, wash, reuse. Love it! Review 16: rating 5.0. Very satisfied with this filter. We love it! Exactly what we expected and a nice, clean tasting coffee. Now if we could just get a plastic free pot! Nice quality product. Review 17: rating 5.0. This one offered stainless steel, so it would be dishwasher-safe, and should last a long time. No plating to wear off. Today it arrived and I tested it right away in the ... Review 18: rating 5.0. I would recommend this to anyone whose dog needs additional vitamins and minerals. Review 20: rating 5.0. Wish I had bought this sooner! I have been trying to reduce the amount of waste I create, so when I was running out of paper coffee filters, I replaced them with this metal filter instead. This was SUCH a great purchase. My coffee has more flavor, it's easier to clean than it was to take out a wet paper filter and hope grounds don't end up everywhere, and it's going to last me a lifetime.
My only small complaint is that it came packaged in plastic, and a big part of getting this...
